title: rosa canina var. sepium
intro: Rosa canina var. sepium, also known as the wild dog rose or European dog-rose, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its hips (seed pods) being particularly valued for their high content of vitamin C and antioxidants, making it a natural remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina var. sepium include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as promote overall health and well-being. In herbal preparations, the hips are often dried and made into teas, syrups, or infusions, which can be taken to support immune function and provide a range of health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved digestion.
